title: Amazon Rolls Out Long-Awaited Dark Mode for Kindle Colorsoft — Here's What It Means for Night Owls
summary: If you're a dedicated nighttime reader, you know the pain of a bright screen jolting your eyes when you just want to switch books. For months, the Kindle Colorsoft — Amazon's first color e-reader — lacked a dark mode, a feature standard on the cheaper Kindle Paperwhite. That omission was a dealbreaker for many, including WIRED's reviewer, who called it the one thing keeping her from using the Colorsoft over other Kindles. Today, Amazon is fixing that. The company announced that dark mode is rolling out to all Colorsoft devices, starting immediately and continuing over the coming weeks. The update transforms the interface by inverting colors — black backgrounds, white text — making it far less intrusive in dim environments. But Amazon didn't stop there. The new dark mode includes customization options, a first for Kindle. You can choose to darken only specific screens. For instance, on the Kindle Scribe Colorsoft, the digital notebook version, you might keep your handwritten notes in light mode while your home screen and books go dark. This granular control is accessed through the Quick Actions menu. Crucially, any color annotations you've made — highlights in ebooks or colorful ink in Scribe notebooks — will retain their original hues in dark mode, so your study notes won't turn into a monochrome mess. The update applies to the entire Colorsoft family: the standard model, the Signature Edition (which adds wireless charging and an auto-adjusting light), the kid-friendly Colorsoft Kids with its protective cover and Amazon Kids+ subscription, and the premium Scribe Colorsoft. If you're impatient, Amazon has posted the update file online for manual installation via desktop computer. This move addresses one of the biggest criticisms of the color Kindle line and could sway fence-sitters. As one reviewer put it, "I'd rather grab my Kindle Paperwhite and know I won't be blinded at 11:45 pm." Now, the Colorsoft can finally compete for that bedside spot.
why_it_matters: Features like dark mode on everyday devices affect your comfort and health — especially if you read or study at night, as reducing eye strain can help you sleep better and protect your vision.

keywords.explanation: Something that has been left out or not included.
Swapping the usual light and dark shades so that backgrounds become dark and text becomes light.
Very detailed and specific control over individual parts of a system.
Notes, highlights, or marks you add to a page or text.
Showing only one color, or black, white, and shades of gray.
People who haven't yet decided which side or product to choose.

questions.question: What was the main complaint that the new dark mode fixes?
options: The screen was too bright in dark environments
It was too heavy to hold
questions.correct_answer: The screen was too bright in dark environments

questions.question: What extra feature does the dark mode include, according to the article?
options: Customization so you can darken only specific screens
questions.correct_answer: Customization so you can darken only specific screens

questions.question: What happens to colorful highlights or notes in dark mode?
Which Kindle model is mentioned as having a feature for note-taking?
How can users get the dark mode update immediately if they don't want to wait?
options: By pressing a secret button on the device
By manually installing the update from Amazon's website via a computer
By subscribing to a special service
questions.correct_answer: By manually installing the update from Amazon's website via a computer

questions.question: Which review source called the missing dark mode a dealbreaker?

background_read: Amazon released the original Kindle in 2007, pioneering e-ink displays that look more like paper and use very little power.
Dark mode (light text on a dark background) became popular in many apps and operating systems because it can reduce eye strain in low light, especially when reading at night.
WIRED (a magazine and website known for in-depth technology and product reviews) originally criticized the Colorsoft for lacking this feature, influencing many buyers.
The Kindle Colorsoft line uses a newer color e-ink technology, which is more complex than the black-and-white screens in older Kindles and took longer to support features like dark mode.
Article_Structure: LEAD: Night reading pain point — bright screen spoils late-night reading.
└─ specific: WIRED reviewer said dark mode absence kept her from using the Colorsoft.
ANNOUNCEMENT: Amazon rolls out dark mode to all Colorsoft devices.
├─ WHAT: Inverts colors (black background, white text) for dim environments.
└─ TIMING: Starts now, continues over weeks.
NEW FEATURE: First-ever granular dark mode customization.
├─ ON SCRIBE: Can keep notes light while home/books go dark.
BENEFIT FOR USERS: Color annotations retain original hues, not monochrome.
WHOLE FAMILY COVERED: Standard, Signature, Kids, and Scribe models.
└─ INSTALL: Update file available online for manual install.
CLOSER: Addresses biggest criticism; reviewer now considers Colorsoft for bedside.

perspectives.description: Night readers finally get a more comfortable experience that matches other Kindles. The ability to keep colorful notes while using dark mode is a smart addition that avoids losing important study highlights.

The fact that a basic feature took so long to arrive on a premium color device frustrates early buyers and raises questions about Amazon’s product priorities. Some might argue that releasing an incomplete device was unfair to loyal customers.

The delay shows the trade-offs when new display technology (color e-ink) is introduced — software must catch up to hardware. This is a pattern seen across many tech products, where the first version lacks features that later models include from day one.

Now that dark mode is solved, the Colorsoft line can focus on other improvements like faster page turns or better color saturation. The competition for the nighttime reading spot will push Amazon to refine the experience further in future updates.
